notes: No machine-readable OpenAPI/Swagger spec is published by CommCare HQ (Dimagi); this spec was hand-authored from the public API docs (ReadTheDocs + Confluence) and cross-checked against the actual paths the adaptor builds in dist/index.cjs. The adaptor is a thin HTTP client: get/post/request map to /a/{domain}/api/v0.5/{resource}/ (Tastypie, v0.5); submit posts XForm XML to /a/{domain}/receiver/{app_id}/; submitXls and bulk('case-data') POST an XLSX to /a/{domain}/importer/excel/bulk_upload_api/; bulk('lookup-table') POSTs an XLSX (field 'file-to-upload') to /a/{domain}/fixtures/fixapi/; fetchReportData GETs /a/{domain}/api/v0.5/configurablereportdata/{report_id}/. Domain is modelled as a server variable. CommCare read endpoints use the Tastypie {meta,objects} envelope, modelled here as reusable component schemas so the mocker can seed Case/Form/User/Group/Fixture collections. Caveats: the receiver actually returns OpenRosa XML (modelled as JSON here for the mocker); the current docs also expose newer versioned paths like /api/case/v1/, but the adaptor pins v0.5. 12 operations. Response field sets are representative, not exhaustive.
